timeline.title: Early Spanish Recognition
timeline.description: The area was recorded by the Spanish as an encomienda named Cagayan.

timeline.title: Spanish Mission Established
timeline.description: Augustinian Recollect missionaries arrived and began settlement development.

timeline.title: Capital of Misamis
timeline.description: Cagayan de Misamis was declared the permanent capital of Misamis Province.

timeline.title: City Charter
timeline.description: The municipality became Cagayan de Oro City under Republic Act No. 521.

facts.title: Geographic Location
facts.description: Cagayan de Oro City is located along the northern coast of Mindanao, facing Macajalar Bay.

facts.title: City Charter
facts.description: It became a chartered city on June 15, 1950 under Republic Act No. 521.

facts.title: Former Name
facts.description: The city was formerly known as Cagayan de Misamis during the Spanish period.

facts.title: Nickname
facts.description: Cagayan de Oro is known as the “City of Golden Friendship.”

facts.title: Economic Role
facts.description: It serves as Northern Mindanao’s main center for trade, education, and transportation.
